rationale: The citing paper reports many moderator-based effects across 10 experiments (e.g., free-will belief, religiosity, political orientation), which typically requires interaction probing and simple-effects testing. The cited paper is a methodological tutorial specifically on moderated regression simple-effects procedures (spotlight/floodlight, Johnson-Neyman), so it is plausibly a methods source rather than an incidental background mention. With a direct DOI match (10.1509/jmr.12.0420), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
